system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)
    // Vertices 0-7
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    // Vertices 8-11
    ( 25   0  25)   // 8
    ( 25   0 -25)   // 9
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 10
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    // Vertices 12-15
    ( 25  -75  25)   // 12
    ( 25  -75 -25)   // 13
    ( 25 -125 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -125  25)   // 15

    (-500  -75  25)   // 16
    (-500  -75 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -125 -25)   // 18
    (-500 -125  25)   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: inlet straight section
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: bend section
    hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: outlet straight section
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 19 18 17)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 4 5 1)
            (3 7 6 2)
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 5 6 2)

            // Block 1 walls
            (7 8 9 6)
            (3 11 10 2)
            (7 3 11 8)
            (6 2 10 9)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 16 17 13)
            (15 19 18 14)
            (12 16 15 19)
            (13 17 18 14)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

{
    version     2.0;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon)"   1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_A_Amodels/runs/squareBend/1/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 49.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block is "inside-out"

The primary error is:
```
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) ... is inside-out
```

This means the vertex ordering in the hex block definition does not follow the right-hand rule convention required by OpenFOAM. When you look at the first block:

```
hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)
```

The bottom face `(0 4 5 1)` and top face `(3 7 6 2)` don't form a valid right-handed hexahedron.

### Root Cause

Looking at the blockMeshDict geometry, there are several problems:

1. **Incorrect vertex ordering** in all three hex blocks
2. **Wrong vertex indices** assigned to blocks — the geometry doesn't match the intended square bend layout
3. **Missing patches** — the boundary definitions reference patches (`innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`) that don't exist in the blockMeshDict's boundary section, while the blockMeshDict only defines `inlet` and `outlet`

### Recommended Fixes

#### Fix the block vertex definitions

The vertex coordinates need to be carefully analyzed. For Block 0 going from x=-50 to x=0 (inlet leg):

- Bottom face should be: z=-25 plane → vertices 2, 1, 5, 6 = `(-50,-25,-25)`, `(-50,25,-25)`, `(0,25,-25)`, `(0,-25,-25)`
- Top face should be: z=+25 plane → vertices 3, 0, 4, 7 = `(-50,-25,25)`, `(-50,25,25)`, `(0,25,25)`, `(0,-25,25)`

So Block 0 should be:
```
hex (2 1 5 6 3 0 4 7)
```

Similarly, reorder all other blocks consistently following the right-hand rule convention.

However, the more fundamental issue is that the **vertex positions themselves** create degenerate or inverted blocks. Looking at the current vertex coordinates:

- Vertex 8: `(25, 0, 25)` and vertex 11: `(25, -50, 25)` — these are at the same x-plane (x=25) but the y-values go from 0 to -50, creating a problematic geometry.
- The outlet vertices (16-19) are at x=-500, which is far behind the inlet at x=-50, making Block 2 geometrically invalid.

**Redesign the vertex layout**: The standard squareBend tutorial uses a different topology where the bend turns in the x-y plane. The vertices need to be restructured so that each block forms a proper convex hexahedron. A correct approach would be:

- Inlet leg runs in +x direction (from x=-50 to x=0)
- Bend goes in -y direction (from y=0 to y=-75 over x=0 to x=25)
- Outlet leg continues in -x direction (from x=25 to x=-500)

Each block needs consistent winding (bottom face CCW when viewed from outside).

#### Add missing boundary patches

The blockMeshDict defines only `inlet` and `outlet` boundaries, but the 0/ files reference `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `front`, and `back`. Either:

- Update the blockMeshDict's `boundary` section to include all six patches matching what the BC files expect, OR
- Simplify the 0/ files to only use `inlet` and `outlet` patches (if a simpler approach is preferred)

Since the user requirement says "follow the same geometry as the tutorial," I recommend adding all necessary boundary patches to blockMeshDict. Based on the squareBend tutorial pattern, the boundary should have `inlet`, `outlet`, and four wall patches (top/bottom and left/right relative to the bend orientation).

---

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points" file

This is a cascading error — since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated, so `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files. Once blockMesh runs successfully, this will be resolved automatically.

---

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **Fix vertex ordering** in all three hex block definitions in `blockMeshDict` to satisfy the right-hand rule
2. **Restructure vertex positions/layout** if needed to ensure all blocks are geometrically valid convex hexahedra
3. **Add all required boundary patches** (`innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `front`, `back`) to the blockMeshDict's boundary section, matching what the 0/ BC files expect
4. After fixing blockMesh, re-run `blockMesh` then `rhoSimpleFoam`</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Fix Block 0 vertex ordering from hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) to hex (2 1 5 6 3 0 4 7) to satisfy right-hand rule; Fix Block 1 vertex ordering from hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2) to hex (6 9 8 7 2 11 10 3) ensuring consistent winding; Fix Block 2 vertex ordering from hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) to hex (13 17 16 12 14 19 18 15) ensuring consistent winding; Add all missing boundary patches: innerWall as type wall with appropriate faces from all three blocks; Add outerWall as type wall with appropriate faces; Add lowerWall as type wall; Add upperWall as type wall; Keep existing inlet and outlet patches unchanged'}, {'file': '0/T', 'changes': 'Remove innerWall, outerWall, lowerWall, upperWall boundary entries and replace with just inlet and outlet patches; Add front patch with type zeroGradient; Add back patch with type zeroGradient; Remove innerWall, outerWall, lowerWall, upperWall entries entirely'}, {'file': '0/U', 'changes': 'Remove upperWall, lowerWall, front, back boundary entries and replace with just inlet and outlet patches; Add innerWall patch with type noSlip; Add outerWall patch with type noSlip; Add lowerWall patch with type noSlip; Add upperWall patch with type noSlip'}, {'file': '0/k', 'changes': 'Remove innerWall, outerWall, lowerWall, upperWall boundary entries and replace with just inlet and outlet patches; Add front patch with type kqRWallFunction and value uniform 1; Add back patch with type kqRWallFunction and value uniform 1'}, {'file': '0/e', 'changes': 'Remove innerWall, outerWall, lowerWall, upperWall boundary entries and replace with just inlet and outlet patches; Add front patch with type zeroGradient; Add back patch with type zeroGradient'}, {'file': '0/nut', 'changes': 'Remove innerWall, outerWall, lowerWall, upperWall boundary entries and replace with just inlet and outlet patches; <user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
